documentation.description: Refresh project-level docs (CLAUDE.md, README.md) to current marketplace state

hook_scripts.description: New PreToolUse/PostToolUse hook scripts in plugins/elixir — block destructive Bash, warn on tooling-flag omissions, doctest IO, shell-eval Elixir

skill_quality.description: Skill-quality pass — progressive disclosure, description optimization, AI-coder-docs scope, includes sync

task.body: Add Does / Does Not scope sections to skills; add a phoenix-setup cross-reference.

task.title: Update Tasks 12-13 for current state
task.body: Refresh the documentation tasks (12, 13) to reflect current marketplace state — they referenced stale plugin names and incomplete skill lists.

task.title: Sync skills with updated includes
task.body: Sync skills with the canonical includes — fix stale content, create new skills.

task.title: Backport skill knowledge to canonical includes
task.body: Rewrite include files with condensed knowledge from skills + references; create the include→skill sync script; fix stale namespace references.

task.title: Block destructive Bash patterns
acceptance_criteria: Each blocked shape returns deny-JSON with a permissionDecisionReason naming the safe alternative
Tests exercise the block patterns and each documented exception — no false positives on `mix deps.unlock --check-unused` or `git rm`
task.body:
Add PreToolUse:Bash hooks that block three command shapes Claude is explicitly told never to run:

- `mix phx.server` — the server is always already running (critical-rules.md § "NEVER START THE PHOENIX SERVER").
- Destructive deps/build commands: `mix deps.clean`, `mix clean`, `mix deps.unlock --all`, `rm -rf _build`, `rm -rf deps` (critical-rules.md § "NEVER RUN DESTRUCTIVE DEPENDENCY COMMANDS"). Must still allow `mix deps.unlock --check-unused` (used by pre-commit-unified.sh).
- Bare `rm` outside of `git rm` (critical-rules.md § "Shell Safety"). Must still allow `git rm`.

Each script outputs the deny-JSON shape with a permissionDecisionReason pointing to the safe alternative.

task.title: Warn on tooling-flag omissions
acceptance_criteria: Warn-only output (never blocks) for missing `--strict --format json` on credo and missing `time` prefix on compile
No false positives on hook scripts' own internal mix invocations
task.body:
Add PreToolUse:Bash hooks that warn (no block) when project-standard flags are missing:

- `mix credo` invoked without `--strict --format json` (development-commands.md).
- `mix compile` invoked without a `time` prefix (development-commands.md).

False-positive guard: don't fire on post-edit-check.sh / pre-commit-unified.sh's own internal `mix credo` / `mix compile` invocations — verify those actually run inside hook scripts rather than via Claude's Bash tool before adding complex matching.

task.title: Warn on doctest IO and untagged temporary code
acceptance_criteria: Warns on IO calls inside @doc heredocs
Warns on deferred-work comment phrases not prefixed with TODO:
No false positives inside string literals or ~s/~S sigils
task.body:
Extend post-edit-check.sh (or add a sibling PostToolUse:Edit|Write|MultiEdit script) with two warn-only checks credo can't see:

- `IO.puts` / `IO.inspect` inside `@doc` heredoc blocks (development-philosophy.md § "No IO in @doc examples").
- `#` comments starting with "For now,", "Currently,", "Temporarily,", "In production,", or "This is a workaround," NOT preceded by `TODO:` (development-philosophy.md § "TODO Comment Requirements").

False-positive guards: never trigger when the patterns appear inside string literals or ~s/~S sigils. Tune for low noise.

task.title: Warn on shell-eval Elixir, prefer Tidewave
acceptance_criteria: New script in plugins/elixir/scripts/, registered in plugins/elixir/hooks/hooks.json under PreToolUse:Bash
Warn-only — must NOT block (seeds and CI scripts share the shape)
4 trigger patterns fire; none of `mix test` / `iex -S mix` / `iex -S mix tidewave` / `mix phx.server` / `mix compile` fire
Warning message names mcp__tidewave__project_eval and mcp__tidewave__get_logs so Claude can call them directly
task.body:
Add a PreToolUse:Bash hook that warns (no block) when Claude is about to run Elixir code through the shell — the cases with a direct mcp__tidewave__project_eval / get_logs equivalent: `mix run -e "<code>"`, `elixir -e "<code>"`, `iex -e "<code>"`, `mix run <path>.exs` (legitimate priv/repo/seeds.exs shares the shape — accepted false positive, called out in the warning).

Why: Tidewave attaches to the same BEAM as the running dev server, so project_eval sees current state and skips the fresh-VM startup `mix run` incurs. The tidewave-guide skill exists but Claude regularly forgets it under shell-muscle-memory.

Reference implementation: a working version exists in hieroglyph/.claude/hookify.prefer-tidewave-over-shell-eval.local.md (12-case regex test included). Port the regex and warning text — the warning includes a replacement table, the recompile() workaround for stale bytecode, and a "legitimate exceptions" footer (seeds, one-shot CI scripts).

task.title: Add [CX] marker to task-prioritization include
task.body: Add a Codex-delegation marker subsection to task-prioritization.md defining the marker, criteria, and workflow. Auto-syncs to roadmap-planning/SKILL.md.

task.body: Add 'DON'T STEAL [CX] TASKS' and 'DON'T AUTO-MERGE PRS' sections to critical-rules.md.

task.body: scripts/sync-agents-md.sh — parses ./CLAUDE.md, inlines all @-imports, writes ./AGENTS.md with an auto-sync banner. --dry-run flag, exit-1 on missing imports.

task.title: SessionStart branch-behind hook
task.body: plugins/elixir/scripts/check-branch-behind-origin.sh — git fetch origin main, warn if behind. Registered as a SessionStart hook.

task.title: commit-review skill in staged-review plugin
task.body: plugins/staged-review/skills/commit-review/SKILL.md — sibling of code-review. Polls Linear for In Review issues, runs the local harness, audits, presents a verdict.

task.title: SessionStart hook for audit detection
task.body: plugins/staged-review/scripts/check-unaudited-commits.sh — detects an unaudited tail via a git log --grep '^audit(' ancestor walk; fires when ≥3 commits sit past the anchor.

task.title: /audit-status roll-up command
task.body: /staged-review:audit-status (with optional --all flag) — read-only per-branch / portfolio-wide audit-drift snapshot.

task.title: Cross-repo audit corpus aggregation
acceptance_criteria: Script in ~/.claude/scripts/audit-corpus.sh (user-scope, not plugin) walks ~/_DATA/code/*/.audit/*.md
Parses frontmatter + Findings tables, aggregates by category and resolution
Output: markdown report with cross-repo trends and candidate include changes
task.body:
Portfolio-level dashboard of audit findings across all repos with .audit/ directories. Strategic, not tactical — answers "what categories of finding recur across my repos?" so the canonical includes can be tightened to prevent future occurrences. Expected output: aggregate frequency table (Category × repo), discuss-design divergence themes, ROADMAP-candidate findings that span repos.

Defer until ≥3 repos have ≥10 audit reports each — otherwise no signal.

task.title: Auto-merge for self-authored worktree PRs
acceptance_criteria: Decision document: which preconditions apply to self-authored vs cloud-agent
Loosen delegation-rules.md § 'DON'T AUTO-MERGE PRS' to cover self-authored if preconditions hold
Update worktree-workflow.md to document the new auto-merge tail (PR-merge → cleanup)
Test: open a self-authored worktree PR, run audit, verify auto-merge fires only when all preconditions hold
task.body:
Extend the cloud-agent auto-merge (scoped to cursor/* / codex/*) to self-authored PRs from worktrees. Needs separate analysis — self-authored work has different blast-radius than reviewed cloud-agent work: no upstream reviewer signal, no agent-PR template, fewer evaluator-separation layers. Likely requires stricter preconditions (mandatory code-review ✅ before commit, mandatory audit-review ✅ on the PR head, [BLOCK-MERGE] label still honored).

Superseded by Task 59 — the GH-native auto-merge model (`gh pr merge <N> --auto --squash --delete-branch` wired at PR-open; branch protection + `[BLOCK-MERGE]` label gate) is now branch-name-agnostic: self-authored worktree PRs and cloud-agent PRs use the same mechanism. No separate self-authored auto-merge path needed.

task.title: Audit re-run on amend / rebase
acceptance_criteria: Detection logic walks .audit/*.md, checks if each sha: frontmatter still exists in git log
On orphan detection, prompt to re-audit OR mark the old report as superseded-by: <new-sha>
Hook into audit-review skill startup so it runs before the next audit pass
Tests cover amend, rebase, and squash-merge
task.body: Detect orphaned .audit/<old-sha>.md files when a commit is amended or rebased. The audit is keyed on commit SHA; rewriting history orphans the report. Offer to re-audit the new SHA and remove the stale file.

task.title: Codex code-mutation re-enablement check
acceptance_criteria: Decision document: criteria for re-enabling Codex code-mutation
Verify the cloud-agent task template wires `gh pr merge <N> --auto --squash --delete-branch` for Codex PRs (per plugins/staged-review/templates/auto-merge.md)
Update agent-dispatch.md and cloud-agent-environments.md to lift the suspension
Run an end-to-end Codex delegation test before declaring re-enabled
task.body: Codex code-mutation is currently suspended per agent-dispatch.md. Track when/if to re-enable so the cloud-agent task template wires `gh pr merge --auto` end-to-end for Codex too. Periodic check on Codex environment improvements (hex.pm reach, mix task availability, Tidewave) — when those change, the suspension's load-bearing reason dissolves.

task.title: Replace zsh-incompatible classification script in audit-review SKILL.md
acceptance_criteria: Replace the inline shell pipeline with a portable bash function (or a small scripts/classify.sh shipped with the skill)
Test on both bash and zsh (default macOS)
Update SKILL.md to call the script with the worktree path arg
task.body:
The classification step in audit-review/SKILL.md shells out a one-liner using `paste -sd+ | bc` for LOC summing. On zsh (default macOS shell) this cascades into "command not found" errors and produces empty output. The SKILL prescription itself should be portable.

Surfaced by: first run of audit-review on the onchain repo (2026-05-09).

task.title: Worktree + clean-tree preconditions in audit-review
acceptance_criteria: Skill checks git rev-parse --show-toplevel against the ~/_DATA/worktrees/<repo>/ pattern; warns/refuses if running in main checkout
Skill checks git status --porcelain empty; refuses if dirty
Both checks documented at the top of SKILL.md as preconditions
Tests cover both refusal paths and the --allow-* overrides
task.body:
audit-review should refuse to run in the main checkout when worktree-workflow.md is loaded (direct the agent to create a worktree first), and refuse to run with a dirty working tree (the audit corpus is a single atomic commit; a dirty tree pollutes scope). Optionally accept --allow-dirty / --allow-main-checkout overrides for repos that opt out of the worktree workflow.

Surfaced by: first run on onchain (2026-05-09) — started in the main checkout with 12 dirty files; user had to interrupt and redirect.

task.title: Audit-corpus-only mode (report without apply)
acceptance_criteria: New flag --report-only in the /audit-review invocation
When set: dispatch Codex, write .audit/<sha>.md reports, but skip all auto-apply steps
Findings flagged in reports as 'Suggested fixes (NOT auto-applied)' rather than applied
Final commit is just the .audit/ directory; no production-code mutations
SKILL.md documents this as the recommended mode for first-run / backfill audits
task.body:
The current skill auto-applies rated-3-10 + actionable + discuss-trivial findings. For backfill / pre-existing audit corpora, users may want report-only: write reports, surface findings, but do not modify project files. Distinct from --no-codex: this gates the application step, not the dispatch step.

Surfaced by: first run on onchain (2026-05-09) — user wanted reports written but no auto-apply because Codex reviews already covered every commit.

task.title: Per-commit Codex output capture in .audit/
acceptance_criteria: Skill writes per-commit Codex output to .audit/<sha>-codex.md (or appends it as a fenced block in the main report)
.audit/<sha>.md cross-references the raw codex output file
Format consistent enough for cross-repo aggregation (Task 40)
task.body:
Codex agent outputs land in a session-scoped tmp dir — durable for the session but lost after compaction. For traceability and re-audit, the raw Codex output should land alongside the .md report — either inline or as a sibling file (.audit/<sha>-codex.md).

Surfaced by: first run on onchain (2026-05-09).

task.title: Deeper rmap integration into delegation + review workflows
acceptance_criteria: agent-dispatch.md routes task-to-prompt rendering through `rmap delegate`
linear-queue.md ROADMAP-fallback flow uses `rmap new` / `rmap next` / `rmap list`
staged-review skills file ceremony-floor candidates via `rmap new --from-stdin` and mutate roadmap state via `rmap status`
sync-skills-from-includes.sh re-run; no drift
task.body:
Stage D of the rmap-mandate migration only did light cross-ref touch-ups on the periphery includes/skills. This task does the deeper integration deferred from that stage:

- `agent-dispatch.md` — wire `rmap delegate <id> --to codex|cursor` into the delegation flow as the canonical way to render a task into a cloud-agent prompt, replacing the hand-built plan-shaped issue spec where the task already lives in `tasks.toml`.
- `linear-queue.md` — make `rmap` the full ROADMAP-fallback substrate: the ROADMAP-fallback flow files via `rmap new --from-stdin` and reads queue state via `rmap next` / `rmap list`, not hand-edited markdown rows.
- `staged-review` skills (`code-review`, `audit-review`) — ceremony-floor "ROADMAP candidate" findings are filed via `rmap new --from-stdin`; `audit-review`'s doc-update step calls `rmap status` / `rmap render` instead of hand-editing `ROADMAP.md`.

Each touched include is canonical (`~/.claude/includes/`) — re-sync skills after editing. The `staged-review` skills are hand-authored — edit directly. Out of scope: changing the rmap CLI itself (tracked in rmap's own roadmap).
task.implemented: agent-dispatch.md routes prompt-rendering through rmap delegate (canonical path with field-to-section mapping: out_of_scope -> ## Out of scope, acceptance_criteria -> ## Acceptance criteria, files_to_modify and env constraints encoded in body or auto-emitted boilerplate; hand-built template demoted to ad-hoc fallback). linear-queue.md ROADMAP-fallback pickup uses rmap next --marker / rmap list --marker; status transitions via rmap status (never hand-edit ROADMAP.md). audit-review SKILL.md line 454 harmonized: rmap status <id> done --implemented "..." signature. code-review already rmap-aligned via Step 2 rmap note; no edits needed. Skills re-synced: 2 SYNCED, 0 drift.

task.title: Propagate the rmap mandate across repos
acceptance_criteria: rmap mandate stated in global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md or a widely-imported include
Active repos audited; each is either migrated to rmap or explicitly noted as exempt
task.body:
The rmap-first posture currently lives in the marketplace's includes (`rmap.md`, `task-prioritization.md`, `task-writing.md`). Propagate "every project uses rmap — `roadmap/tasks.toml` canonical, `ROADMAP.md` rendered" so it actually reaches every repo:

- Roll the mandate into the global `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (or a widely-imported include) so new repos inherit it.
- Audit other active repos' `CLAUDE.md` files and roadmaps; migrate hand-edited `ROADMAP.md` files to `roadmap/tasks.toml` per the migration procedure in `rmap.md`, or note which repos are intentionally exempt.

This is the "make it actually standard, not just documented" follow-through.

task.title: Propagate batched-execution rule into task-driver skill
acceptance_criteria: Step 2 output proposes batches when shortlist tasks are independent
Filed plans for multi-batch work include ⏸ CHECKPOINT markers as lines of their own (not prose)
Commit one-liners appear at checkpoints where a commit is the next step
Section cross-references workflow-philosophy.md § Batched Execution
task.body: Update `plugins/task-driver/skills/task-driver/SKILL.md` to apply the Batched Execution rule from `~/.claude/includes/workflow-philosophy.md`. Pickup Mode Step 2 (the Parallel Opportunities display) should escalate to proposing a batch + subagent fan-out when the shortlist contains independent tasks. Plan-and-File mode (P1-P6) should render ⏸ CHECKPOINT markers and commit one-liners as first-class structural elements in filed plans when the plan is multi-batch. Reference the canonical rule rather than duplicating it.

task.title: Propagate batched-execution rule into workflow-generator templates
acceptance_criteria: plan-template renders ⏸ CHECKPOINT as a line of its own when the plan is multi-batch
implement-template stops at ⏸ CHECKPOINT and emits a commit one-liner if commit is next
oneshot-template no longer blanket-forbids parallel phases
Templates cross-reference workflow-philosophy.md § Batched Execution
task.body: Update `plugins/elixir-workflows/skills/workflow-generator/templates/{plan,implement,oneshot}-template.md` to apply the Batched Execution rule. plan-template renders ⏸ CHECKPOINT markers as first-class structural elements in phased plans; implement-template's phase-loop honors them as /compact STOPs and fans out disjoint same-batch work to subagents; oneshot-template's 'Sequential Execution Notes' is reconciled — disjoint phases MAY fan out, dependent phases stay sequential. May split into one task per template at pickup if oversized.

task.title: Generalize agent-dispatch /compact section as instance of canonical rule
acceptance_criteria: agent-dispatch.md cross-references workflow-philosophy.md § Batched Execution as the general form
Existing /compact-between-batches rule still reads correctly
Sync to agent-dispatch skill is clean (no drift)
task.body: Rework `~/.claude/includes/agent-dispatch.md` § 'Batch Sizing and Pacing' so its /compact-between-batches language is framed as the delegation-specific *instance* of the canonical rule in workflow-philosophy.md § Batched Execution. Vocabulary stays — this is where 'batch' originates. Run `scripts/sync-skills-from-includes.sh` to propagate to the cloud-delegation:agent-dispatch skill.

task.title: Document /batch + rmap next-bundle integration with Batched Execution
acceptance_criteria: rmap.md mentions batch as derived layer of next-bundle output
flow-review.md notes /batch → flow-review synergy + commit-review gate guard
Skills sync cleanly (rmap, flow-review)
task.body: Add cross-references and integration docs: in `~/.claude/includes/rmap.md`, document that a 'batch' is a derived dependency layer of a next-bundle task set (no new rmap command — batch-derivation is the executor's job, computed from depends_on edges). In `~/.claude/includes/flow-review.md`, note that /batch output (N PRs) feeds the merge-train and that batch-produced PRs still route through commit-review (don't bypass the pre-commit gate). Touches rmap.md and flow-review.md; run sync.

task.title: Align audit-review/flow-review checkpoint vocabulary with canonical ⏸ CHECKPOINT
acceptance_criteria: audit-review SKILL.md uses ⏸ CHECKPOINT marker in its checkpoint-rendering language
flow-review cross-references the canonical Batched Execution rule
task.body: Audit-review's existing `_in_progress_<range>.md` STOP-checkpoint predates the canonical rule. Align: ensure `plugins/staged-review/skills/audit-review/SKILL.md` uses the ⏸ CHECKPOINT glyph as the rendered marker (the on-disk filename stays — it's the resume artifact), and that flow-review.md adds a cross-reference to workflow-philosophy.md § Batched Execution where appropriate.

task.title: Evaluate batch-worker subagent definition
acceptance_criteria: Decision is documented (either a new agent file lands, or workflow-philosophy.md gains a brief why-no-batch-worker note)
If shipped: subagent works in a fan-out test with isolation: worktree
Plugin's plugin.json + SKILLS.md + README catalogs updated if a new agent ships
task.body: Decide whether to ship a reusable worktree-isolated batch-worker subagent definition (in a plugin's `agents/` dir, per the Claude Code sub-agents docs) for disjoint-batch fan-out, versus continuing to lean on built-in Explore / general-purpose / codex:codex-rescue. Subagent definitions can pre-set isolation: worktree, tool restrictions, and a focused system prompt; against, plugin-shipped subagents can't use hooks/mcpServers/permissionMode. If shipping, place under plugins/task-driver/agents/ or a new plugin. If not, document the decision in workflow-philosophy.md § Batched Execution as a footnote.

task.title: Evaluate rmap next-bundle --batches flag (cross-repo)
acceptance_criteria: Decision is recorded (link to rmap-repo issue or PR)
If implemented in rmap: rmap.md gets a follow-up edit documenting --batches
task.body: Cross-repo tracker — rmap is a separate binary. Evaluate whether `rmap next-bundle` should grow a `--batches` flag that groups a bundle's tasks into dependency layers (topological sort by depends_on) and emits them grouped in the JSON. The alternative is leaving batch-derivation entirely to the consuming executor. If the flag lands, executors can read pre-grouped batches without reimplementing topo-sort. Decision + implementation happens in the rmap repo; this task is a placeholder so the question doesn't get lost.

task.title: Decouple audit-review from commit-review; default to deferred/batched
acceptance_criteria: commit-review SKILL.md no longer chains audit-review post-merge (Step 15 removed or reframed)
delegation-rules.md auto-merge tail ends at `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch` + branch cleanup; no audit-review chain
worktree-workflow.md documents audit-review as a deferred batched pass, with the SessionStart hook + audit-status skill as the surfacing path
Existing `check-unaudited-commits.sh` threshold (≥3) reviewed — adjust if needed so the new model triggers reliably without nag
Decision recorded in the task on background-fire-and-forget vs manual + hook (recommend manual + hook)
Skills sync clean (`./scripts/sync-skills-from-includes.sh --dry-run` shows expected diffs)
Test: merge a PR, verify no audit-review fires synchronously; verify SessionStart hook flags it next session
out_of_scope: Task 41 (self-authored auto-merge preconditions) — separate concern about whether to auto-merge, not when audit runs
Task 46 (--report-only mode) — about invocation mode, not trigger model
Implementing a true background-runner (e.g. a `claude` subprocess with --bare); recommendation in task body is to defer that
task.body:
Remove the synchronous `Skill(audit-review)` chain from `commit-review` Step 15 (and the equivalent post-merge step in `worktree-workflow.md` / `delegation-rules.md`). Audit-review becomes a deferred, range-capable pass triggered by the existing SessionStart hook (`check-unaudited-commits.sh`) and the `audit-status` skill — not a per-merge tail.

Rationale: audit-review is hygiene + bookkeeping, not a correctness gate. The current chain costs minutes per merge (Codex dispatch + apply + commit) and serializes N merges into N audit passes; batching one pass over a range is strictly cheaper. The infra is already in place — SessionStart hook flags the tail at ≥3 unaudited, `Skill(audit-review)` accepts a range, `audit-status` surfaces it.

Decision: **manual + SessionStart hook** (not background fire-and-forget). Background subprocesses in the same session can't spawn their own subagents (harness rule), output isn't visible to the user, and the existing hook + audit-status surfacing path is already idempotent and proven. Background-runner can be reconsidered if the hook proves insufficient in practice.

Touched docs:
- `plugins/staged-review/skills/commit-review/SKILL.md` — drop Step 15 chain, update flow diagram, update the comparison table
- `plugins/staged-review/skills/audit-review/SKILL.md` — collapse 4 chain triggers to 2 (SessionStart hook + manual)
- `~/.claude/includes/delegation-rules.md` § 'DON'T AUTO-MERGE PRS' — auto-merge tail ends at branch cleanup
- `~/.claude/includes/worktree-workflow.md` § 'After PR Merge' — reframed as deferred
- `~/.claude/includes/dev-lifecycle.md` — phase 6 trigger column
- `~/.claude/includes/linear-queue.md`, `agent-pr-review.md`, `agent-dispatch.md`, `flow-review.md` — chain references updated
- `CLAUDE.md`, `SKILLS.md`, `README.md`, `plugins/staged-review/README.md` — skill table rows

task.title: Harden audit-review SKILL.md (preconditions, report-only, codex capture, classification, checkpoint vocab)
acceptance_criteria: Preconditions: skill checks git rev-parse --show-toplevel against ~/_DATA/worktrees/<repo>/; refuses with --allow-main-checkout override
Preconditions: skill checks git status --porcelain empty; refuses with --allow-dirty override
Preconditions: both checks documented at the top of SKILL.md
--report-only flag: dispatches Codex, writes .audit/<sha>.md reports, skips all auto-apply steps
--report-only flag: findings flagged as 'Suggested fixes (NOT auto-applied)' in reports
--report-only flag: final commit is .audit/ directory only; no production-code mutations
Codex capture: per-commit raw output lands at .audit/<sha>-codex.md (or fenced block in the main report)
Codex capture: main .audit/<sha>.md cross-references the raw output
Codex capture: format is consistent enough for Task 40 cross-repo aggregation
Classification: portable bash function or scripts/classify.sh replaces the paste|bc pipeline
Classification: tested on both bash and zsh (default macOS)
Checkpoint vocab: SKILL.md uses ⏸ CHECKPOINT glyph as the rendered marker
Checkpoint vocab: flow-review.md cross-references workflow-philosophy.md § Batched Execution
Tests cover refusal paths, --allow-* overrides, --report-only flow, and classification on both shells
task.body:
Consolidates tasks 44, 45, 46, 47, 54 — all touch `plugins/staged-review/skills/audit-review/SKILL.md` and most were surfaced by the same first run on the onchain repo (2026-05-09). One PR, one sync, one set of tests.

Five concerns, all on the same skill:

1. **Worktree + clean-tree preconditions (was Task 45):** Refuse to run in the main checkout when worktree-workflow.md is loaded; refuse to run with a dirty working tree. Accept `--allow-dirty` / `--allow-main-checkout` overrides. Surfaced when the first run started in the main checkout with 12 dirty files; user had to interrupt and redirect.

2. **`--report-only` mode (was Task 46):** New flag that dispatches Codex and writes `.audit/<sha>.md` reports but skips all auto-apply. Distinct from `--no-codex` (which gates dispatch); this gates application. Recommended mode for first-run / backfill audits where Codex reviews already covered the commits. Surfaced when user wanted reports but no auto-apply because Codex had already reviewed every commit.

3. **Per-commit Codex output capture (was Task 47):** Codex outputs land in a session-scoped tmp dir today — durable in-session, lost after compaction. Write the raw output to `.audit/<sha>-codex.md` (or inline as a fenced block); the main report cross-references it. Format must be consistent enough for the cross-repo aggregation in Task 40.

4. **Replace zsh-incompatible classification script (was Task 44):** The classification step shells out a one-liner using `paste -sd+ | bc` for LOC summing. On zsh (default macOS shell) it cascades into "command not found" errors and produces empty output. Replace with a portable bash function or a small `scripts/classify.sh` shipped with the skill.

5. **Align checkpoint vocabulary with canonical ⏸ CHECKPOINT (was Task 54):** Audit-review's existing `_in_progress_<range>.md` STOP-checkpoint predates the canonical Batched Execution rule. Ensure SKILL.md uses the ⏸ CHECKPOINT glyph as the rendered marker (the on-disk filename stays — it's the resume artifact). Add cross-reference from `flow-review.md` to `workflow-philosophy.md` § Batched Execution where appropriate.

All edits target the same SKILL.md plus (potentially) a sibling `scripts/classify.sh` and a one-paragraph touch on `flow-review.md`. Re-sync skills after editing canonical includes.

task.title: Merge commit-review into audit-review; pre-merge becomes GitHub-native auto-merge
acceptance_criteria: plugins/staged-review/skills/commit-review/ removed
audit-review SKILL.md has Steps 4.5, 5d, 9-extension, 12.5
plugins/staged-review/templates/auto-merge.md exists with gh pr merge --auto + block-merge-gate.yml content
grep -rn commit-review over repo + canonical includes returns zero load-bearing hits (CHANGELOG.md prose + historical-task bodies + plugin description changelog mentions allowed)
marketplace.json staged-review description rewritten to two-tier; metadata.version 1.4.0 → 1.5.0
Five-phase lifecycle in CLAUDE.md, SKILLS.md, README.md, dev-lifecycle skill (synced from include)
Task 41 status = 'superseded'
CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry present
out_of_scope: Task 41 (folded into this task — Task 41 status superseded; see this body)
Designing a tiny watcher script instead of GH-native (rejected; GH-native is one flag)
task.body:
Delete commit-review skill (901 LOC). Pre-merge phase becomes zero-Claude: bot ensemble + CI gate the merge; `gh pr merge --auto --squash --delete-branch` set when PR opens. Bot-comment triage, Linear close-out, acceptance-criteria verification, push-back-default fold into audit-review post-merge. Six-phase lifecycle collapses to five.

Rationale: commit-review's harness ceremony (~100k tokens/PR) paid premium for marginal incremental signal over CodeRabbit + Copilot + Codex GH bot + CI. Cloud-agent wake-up loops just relocate the bill (sandbox spin-up + their harness). Both shapes are unjustifiable.

The `[BLOCK-MERGE]` label remains the manual escape hatch for critical-tier PRs that need pre-merge human review.

Supersedes Task 41 — the GH-native auto-merge mechanism is branch-name-agnostic, so self-authored worktree PRs and cloud-agent PRs use the same path. Task 41's separate "auto-merge for self-authored worktree PRs" investigation is no longer needed.

Affected files: plan at ~/.claude/plans/vectorized-seeking-jellyfish.md.
task.implemented: Deleted plugins/staged-review/skills/commit-review/ (901 LOC). Extended audit-review SKILL.md with Step 4.5 (PR+Linear comment resolution), Step 5a bot-as-input integration, Step 5d (3-reasoner bot-finding triage table), Step 9 acceptance-criteria extension, Step 12.5 (Linear close-out). Created plugins/staged-review/templates/auto-merge.md. Updated marketplace.json (1.4.0→1.5.0; staged-review desc three-tier→two-tier; dev-lifecycle desc six-phase→five-phase). 11 canonical includes purged of commit-review refs; delegation-rules.md § Commit-Review Header section deleted. Repo catalogs: CLAUDE.md (skill count 41→40), SKILLS.md, README.md, plugins/staged-review/README.md (commit-review section replaced with GH-native pointer), plugins/dev-lifecycle/README.md + commands + plugin.json (1.0.2→1.1.0), plugins/staged-review/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (1.20.0→2.0.0 major bump for skill deletion). Non-synced skill bodies patched: code-review (Phase Awareness table 6→5), task-driver (six-phase chain + Step 3.5 router refs), elixir-ci-harness (commit-review CI-as-gate → GH-native auto-merge). ROADMAP.md Phase 9 + Phase 10 methodology preambles updated; Future Scope rows referencing commit-review removed. CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry. Task 41 superseded. Tasks 43, 48 bodies refined.

task.title: dev-discipline plugin: pause-and-pick before rmap new / new-task TOML edits / long sleeps
acceptance_criteria: New plugin directory `plugins/dev-discipline/` with `hooks/hooks.json` and `scripts/*.sh`
Three hooks wired and registered in the marketplace manifest
Each script reads stdin JSON, returns exit 0 with a reminder (additionalContext or stderr), does NOT block
rmap-new-pause fires on `rmap new` and `rmap new --from-stdin`, does NOT fire on `rmap list`, `rmap show`, etc.
tasks-toml-new-task-pause fires when an Edit/Write/MultiEdit on `*/roadmap/tasks.toml` adds `[[task]]` to the diff; does NOT fire on status/marker edits or non-tasks.toml files
polling-warn fires on `sleep 30`, `sleep 120`, `until ...; do sleep 2; done`; does NOT fire on `sleep 5` (short bridge sleeps are legitimate)
README or moduledoc-equivalent in the plugin describing the philosophy (pause-and-pick, hookify rationale)
out_of_scope: Hard blocks — all three are reminders only
Migration of existing harness-side polling block (separate concern, lives there)
Memory-system changes (this hook supersedes the need for sharper memory)
task.body:
Add a new plugin `dev-discipline` with three PreToolUse hooks that fire soft reminders (non-blocking) at the moments when Claude tends to choose ceremony over inline fix:

1. **rmap-new-pause** — PreToolUse:Bash, matcher `^rmap\s+new(\s|$)`. Reminder text: "rmap new gate — is the finding fixable in this commit? In-scope → fix inline. Cross-session / blocks a different PR → file. Pause and pick."

2. **tasks-toml-new-task-pause** — PreToolUse:Edit|Write|MultiEdit. Path filter: file ends in `roadmap/tasks.toml`. Content filter: the new/added content introduces a `[[task]]` block not present before. Same reminder text as #1. Closes the workaround corridor where Claude bypasses the Bash matcher by editing the TOML directly.

3. **polling-warn** — PreToolUse:Bash. Matcher `\bsleep\s+([2-9]\d|\d{3,})\b` OR `until\s+.*;\s*do\s+sleep`. Reminder text: "No polling. Long-running dispatch → return to the user. Use a Monitor for an event, not a sleep." Soft warn — the harness-side rule is the hard wall; this is the cross-repo cousin.

All three are reminders, not blocks (exitCode 0, output via `additionalContext` JSON or stderr — non-blocking). Modeled on the existing `marketplace-hygiene/scripts/block-skill-edits.sh` shape but emitting `additionalContext`, not `deny`.

Source of the recommendation: hookify:conversation-analyzer run against transcript `/Users/efries/.claude/projects/-Users-efries--DATA-code-harness/aa46fc79-eaf4-4bd8-a826-8bbd94667770.jsonl` (Task 53/54 closeout session in harness). User explicitly flagged the close-2-open-2 pattern as cross-session, not just this-session.

Why memory wasn't enough: `feedback_dogfood-task-spawn-rate` names the *outcome* ("ratio must stay positive") but doesn't fire at the trigger word. Hooks fire deterministically at the moment of action; memory is fuzzy recall.

Sister-plugin reference: see `plugins/marketplace-hygiene/scripts/block-skill-edits.sh` for the `INPUT=$(cat) | jq -r` pattern and the `emit_*` helpers.
task.implemented: Shipped plugins/dev-discipline (3 PreToolUse hooks: rmap-new-pause, tasks-toml-new-task-pause, polling-warn) + marketplace.json registration. PR #9 squash-merged on main.
